museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: A month of settling in: the European N64 is no longer theoretical, PlayStation keeps the shelf broad, and Japan introduces force feedback with Star Fox 64.
museum.curatorNote: April is anchored by the Rumble Pak/Star Fox 64 Japanese moment and by UK post-launch N64 ownership rather than a crowded set of British release dates.

sections.title: News
sections.subtitle: After the March launch rush, April is about what the new machines started to mean.

items.title: Rumble becomes the next controller promise
items.body: The Rumble Pak launched in Japan with Star Fox 64, making force feedback part of the N64 story months before many UK players encountered it through Lylat Wars.
items.sourceNote: Guinness World Records lists the Rumble Pak as launching in April 1997 to coincide with Star Fox 64.

items.title: UK N64 owners settle into the launch library
items.body: The post-launch month was not just about new games. It was about proving that Mario 64, Pilotwings 64, and Shadows of the Empire could sustain a new console.
items.sourceNote: European launch titles verified from March 1997; April note describes post-launch context.

items.title: PlayStation remains the wide shelf
items.body: Nintendo had novelty; Sony had breadth. In UK shops, the PlayStation's CD-ROM library and magazine demo discs kept it feeling like the safer everyday machine.
items.sourceNote: Included as UK retail context rather than a single dated event.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: April's release story is mostly regional: Japan sees the tactile future first.

items.title: Star Fox 64
items.body: Japanese release window for the game that introduced Rumble Pak support and would become Lylat Wars in Europe.
items.sourceNote: Rumble Pak launch context ties Star Fox 64 to April 1997 in Japan.

items.title: Super Mario 64
items.body: Still the defining UK N64 experience in the month after launch.
items.sourceNote: Not an April release; included as active UK shelf context.

items.title: Diablo
items.body: Still the PC game that made home networking and dungeon repetition feel newly social.
items.sourceNote: January release; included for ongoing 1997 PC momentum.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: The year starts to move from polygons to sensation.

items.title: Rumble Pak
items.body: Force feedback made the controller feel less like a window and more like a small machine reacting in the hands.
items.sourceNote: April 1997 Japanese launch context from Guinness World Records.

items.title: PlayStation memory cards
items.body: While Nintendo sold cartridge permanence, PlayStation owners learned to manage save blocks like a small filing cabinet.

sections.title: Magazine Covers
sections.subtitle: Rumble and import reports made hardware feel narrative.

items.title: Import Star Fox coverage
items.headline: Rumble before Britain
items.coverImageAlt: Reconstructed Nintendo magazine-style cover image standing in for April 1997.
items.curatorNote: Import coverage taught UK readers about rumble before many could try it.
items.body: Import coverage taught UK readers about rumble before many could try it.

items.title: PlayStation demo-disc routine
items.headline: The monthly playable sampler
items.coverImageAlt: Reconstructed PlayStation magazine-style cover image standing in for April 1997.
items.curatorNote: The demo disc remained a practical answer to the question, what should I play next?
items.body: The demo disc remained a practical answer to the question, what should I play next?

items.title: QuakeWorld kept the server list alive
items.body: PC players were learning to think in pings, addresses, and late-night connections.

items.title: Console news still came on paper
items.body: For the N64 and PlayStation, magazine pages remained the most reliable monthly feed.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: April feels like the month after a birthday: the present is real now, and you are learning its limits.

items.title: The N64 had to prove depth
items.body: Mario was magnificent, but owners were already asking what came next.

items.title: Rumble sounded strange until you felt it
items.body: Force feedback was hard to describe on paper, which made it perfect magazine mythology.

sources.label: Rumble Pak launch context
sources.note: Used for April 1997 Rumble Pak/Star Fox 64 context.
